slide_1_Cover: Smartphone-Based Body Size Tracking AI Platform
slide_2_Idea_Problem_Solution_Value: Tracks visible body shape via circumference not abstract body fat percentage — users see measurable weekly size changes that match their mirror and clothing experience
Daily home tracking at $2-3/month versus monthly InBody clinic visits at $50+ — eliminates both cost and access barriers entirely with pure software delivery model
ISAK L2 anthropometry expertise combined with AI engineering delivers validated 4-6% error — patent filing in progress positions circumference as the daily fitness metric
Smartphone photo scan uses computer vision AI to capture body circumference at 4-6% error — enabling affordable daily tracking without any clinic visit or hardware needed
B2B dashboard with 3D avatar and progress charts helps gyms, trainers, and weight-loss centers boost member retention — coach workflow integrates into existing operations
Automated circumference tracking of waist, hips, and arms visualizes what users emotionally care about seeing change — progress charts replace manual tape and guesswork
Weight-loss users care about visible size changes in waist, hips, and arms but have no affordable daily tracking tools — InBody and DEXA are clinic-only at $50+ each
InBody and DEXA measure body fat percentage, not the circumference changes users actually want — expensive, clinic-only devices inaccessible for daily or weekly home use
Manual tape measurement is inconsistent across sessions, smart scales estimate poorly, and photo comparison is subjective — no trusted automated daily method exists today
slide_3_Market_1_3_TAM_SAM_SOM_+_Why_Now: Korea: InBody(50% market share) brand awareness creates body-composition literacy; 15,548 fitness facilities growing 5.2% YoY — initial B2B wedge market exists
GLP-1 patient reach tripled to ~12M globally; H&F app downloads hit 3.6B in 2024 with Medical Tracking up 43% — body-tracking demand structural
Smartphone-only model needs zero hardware; GLP-1, weight loss, body recomp universal — but distribution and trust are the real expansion bottleneck
Global digital health is the fastest-expanding consumer tech category
Fitness and mobile health apps — smartphone penetration meets AI-powered body tracking demand
Fitness early adopters, GLP-1 users, and chain gym partnerships
AI adoption and mobile-first health solutions are reshaping how consumers monitor and manage their bodies — demand for personalized health data is accelerating
Market triples from $288B (2024) to $946B by 2030 — the growth window is open now
The $10.6B fitness app market is Namatan's primary segment — growing 3× to $33.6B by 2033 on the back of AI body tracking and wearable integration
Smartphone-native users already expect AI-driven personalization — the infrastructure is there, the right product is not
GLP-1 users represent a fast-growing, underserved segment with acute need for body composition tracking beyond the scale
Chain gym partnerships create a scalable B2B2C distribution channel — reaching high-intent users at the point of workout
slide_4_Market_2_3_Competition: Hardware body composition analyzer in 110+ countries; strongest benchmark — Namatan complements daily tracking, not replaces
Two-photo 3D body scan with telehealth and weight-loss use cases; US patent filed, GDPR/HIPAA — Namatan novelty limited
Full-body selfie composition plus AI coaching with $11M+ raised; broader product ambition — most direct smartphone competitor
Consumer 3D body visualizer with 14+ measurements including waist, hips, arms — already implements the visible-change UX
Circumference-first tracking targets what users emotionally care about — competitors optimize for body fat % which users find abstract and unactionable
Daily home tracking at $2-3/month vs clinic-only InBody — eliminates hardware cost and access friction, pure software margin with zero COGS scaling
Category is validated but crowded; moat must come from longitudinal data, coach workflow lock-in, and privacy trust — not from technology novelty alone
slide_5_Market_3_3_GTM_Pricing_BM_Scale: Zero hardware cost — InBody replacement at fraction of price
B2B API $1,200-6,000/year per integration; usage-based licensing
Dual revenue: B2C freemium subscription + B2B SaaS per-seat licensing for coaches and centers
B2B multiplier: one gym partnership onboards hundreds of users at once with near-zero marginal cost
Stage 1: Korea B2C fitness users and B2B trainers via freemium app — target gym-goers through partner networks and build brand in InBody-literate market
Stage 2: Gym chain partnerships and GLP-1 clinic integrations across Korea — white-label tracking dashboard scales from individual trainers to chain accounts
Stage 3: Global API integration with fitness and diet apps as embedded body-tracking feature — shift from direct sales to B2B2C distribution model
Pure software with zero hardware cost — user growth expands margins without COGS increase; freemium funnel achieves 5-8% paid conversion rate
B2B multiplier: one gym chain deal onboards hundreds of users automatically — efficient CAC amortization with built-in distribution channel
Korea to SEA to US sequential expansion via API integrations with fitness app ecosystem — reuses core platform with minimal localization cost
slide_6_Team_1_4_Composition_&_Roles: CEO Mohsen has 10yr body composition expertise as ISAK L2 anthropometrist; CTO Vahid brings 10yr full-stack and AI engineering — skills span domain science, AI, and product execution
Introduced through mutual friend; aligned on complementary skills and circumference-first approach through online meetings before co-founding — Berlin base enables cost-efficient dev
CEO drives product vision, domain science, GTM strategy, and fundraising; CTO builds platform architecture, AI engine, and computer vision pipeline from Berlin with clear role separation
Narrowed from broad body composition to circumference-focused tracking after Bootcamp 1 customer feedback — demonstrated willingness to kill features and focus on validated user pain point

Introduced via mutual friend in late 2025; aligned through online meetings on complementary skills — Vahid's Berlin base enables cost-efficient development remotely
CTO not in Antler program; no co-founder interaction within office space — language barrier flagged during onboarding; Mohsen managing all primary Korea communication
AI body composition from smartphone photos using computer vision. Bootcamp 1 Jiho surfaced unclear core customer — pivoted to fitness-specific circumference tracking
Bootcamp 6 Jiho trackout — nailed core problem; circumference vs composition distinction is the key gap. Validated demand from gym trainers and GLP-1 clinic operators

slide_8_Team_3_4_Evaluation: Mutual friend intro. CEO Seoul, CTO Berlin part-time elsewhere. Equity undefined. Remote-only, low cohesion signals
Locked in pilot programs with at least 2 different clinics/firms. One in discussions around exclusive distribution. Need to confirm willingness to pay.
Bootcamp 1 Red to Bootcamp 6 Trackout. Problem definition refined via customer feedback. Hustling Korea sales despite language gap
CEO ISAK L2 with 10yr body measurement expertise. Circumference vs body-fat gap deeply understood. Clear ICP defined
CEO domain expertise strong. CTO 10yr full-stack plus prior AI image app. The technology works but defensibity uncertain.
Smartphone circumference focus is InBody paradigm shift. GLP-1 timing excellent. Novel body tracking approach
Based on weekly founder retrospectives and feedback
slide_9_Team_4_4_Strengths_&_Improvement: Strengths
● Smartphone body scan category is validated
Multiple peer-reviewed studies confirm feasibility; 3DLOOK, Spren, MeThreeSixty already commercial — category tech risk is low
● GLP-1 demand creates structural tailwind
Patient reach tripled to ~12M globally; lean mass monitoring during weight loss increasingly critical — demand is structural
● CEO domain expertise in body measurement
ISAK L2 anthropometrist with 10yr experience; problem-founder fit strong — understands measurement pain from practitioner side
Areas for Improvement
● Latecomer to already validated category
3DLOOK, Spren, MeThreeSixty, and Amazon Halo all preceded — Namatan is not category creator but follower in crowded field
● No confirmed paying customers yet
No revenue, no paid validation, no retention data; willingness-to-pay and scan habit unproven
● CTO part-time remote with no equity
Employed elsewhere in Berlin; equity conditional on funding; not in-program - tech leader departure risk is high (however the product is not dependent on the CTO)
● Amazon Halo proves tech alone insufficient
Validated in 1,200-person study then shut down 2023; privacy, habit, and channel fit must all align — accuracy alone insufficient
Team (4/4)
Summary

● Equity has been discussed but not finalized, pending Antler’s investment decision; the final split will depend on each founder’s involvement and time commitment given current location and availability differences
Founder Alignment & Equity Structure
slide_10_Investor_Assessment: Rapidly growing, very large market cap
Namatan is required to expand into adjacent business models in order to capture it
Validated category with various products in the market - crowded
Namatan aims to provide certified practitioner-level data based on scientific standards
However, anthropometry protocols are public and not a proprietary science
Moat must come from longitudinal data, workflow lock-in, and privacy trust, not from technology novelty
Accuracy of their product is on par with existing hardware based solutions on benchmark tests
However, technical moat is fragile and have not validated customer willingness to pay yet
No major hurdles to global expansion assuming they are able to penetrate the South Korea market where InBody has 50% market share
Mohsen has unusual hustle for a scientist, he was able to secure sales leads despite not speaking Korean
Unsure about CTO, haven’t really spoken to him, however I don’t see this as too much a risk as the actual software implementation is not a differentiator

Large, fast-growing market with a clear wedge, but a crowded category where differentiation is not defensible at the technology level. Core bet is on execution: converting early B2B interest into real usage and proving repeatable user behavior, not just measurement accuracy. Upside is significant if they can take share from InBody in Korea, validating both distribution and habit formation for global expansion. Inevitabile solution / Solo founder risk
